description_long: This is a large, rustic log cabin with a sprawling, multi-sectioned layout, set on a landscaped plot with pathways, trees, and a grassy yard. The structure features a prominent gable roof with deep overhangs and multiple peaks, constructed from dark brown shingles. The exterior walls are made of thick, rounded logs, giving the building a robust and traditional appearance. Several windows of varying sizes are distributed along the facades, and there are multiple visible doors, including a main entrance flanked by a covered porch. The property includes a driveway, a fenced area, and decorative landscaping elements such as coniferous trees and stone-bordered paths. The overall design suggests a luxurious, high-end log cabin suitable for residential use in a suburban or semi-rural neighborhood.
description: A large, multi-wing log cabin with a gabled roof, landscaped yard, and multiple porches and entrances, featuring rustic log construction and decorative trees.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch, three coniferous trees, and a wide stone path leading up to the cabin.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the cabin, a side entrance, and a fenced area on the right side of the property.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying multiple windows, a secondary entrance, and a deck or patio area, with the fenced section visible on the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing another covered porch, winding paths, and additional landscaping with trees and grass.
attribute_roof_type: multi-gable roof with deep overhangs, constructed from dark brown asphalt shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Multiple rectangular windows of varying sizes are distributed asymmetrically along the front and rear facades, with some grouped in pairs and others spaced individually; the arrangement is irregular but balanced across the structure.
attribute_door_placement: Main entrance door is centered on the front facade beneath the porch; additional doors are located on both side facades and at the rear, providing access to porches and patios.
